Q2 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A household refrigerator is maintained at a temperature of 3°C. Everytime the door is opened, warm material is placed inside, introducing an average of 400 kJ, but making only a small change in the temperature of the refrigerator. The door is opened 10 times a day, and the refrigerator operates at 20% of the ideal COP. The cost of work is  per kWh. Atmosphere is at 27°C,
monthly bill for the refrigerator will be (Approx to nearest integer)

NAT
Correct answer: 14
Solution

14 (13 - 15)

 
Q3 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A heat engine is used to drive a heat pump. The heat transfer from the heat engine and from the heat pump are used to heat the water circulating through the radiators of a building. The efficiency of the heat engine is 30% and the COP of heat pump is 5. Ratio of rate of heat transfer to the circulating water to the heat transfer to the heat engine is

 
Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

 
Q4 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A compressor takes in 600 kg/minutes of air at 1 bar and 10°C. The work input provided to compressor is 1000 kW. Velocity at the inlet of compressor is 45 m/sec and at the exit of compressor is 47 m/sec. Temperature at the exit of compressor is 60°C. The rate of heat flow is ______ kJ/sec.

NAT
Correct answer: -496.58
Solution

-496.58 (-498 - -495)

 
Q5 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

An inventor claims that he has developed a heat engine which extract 2000 kJ and 1000 kJ from reservoir at 900 K and 400 K respectively and rejects 500 kJ and 200 kJ as heat to reservoir at 200 K and 100 K respectively, which of the following statement is correct regarding heat engine?

 
Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

 
Q6 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Two vessels, A and B, each of volume 2 m3 may be connected by a tube of negligible volume, as shown in figure. Vessel A contains air at 0.8 MPa, 97°C, while vessel B contains air at 0.2 MPa, 200°C. Assuming the mixing to adiabatic, change in entropy is ______ kJ/K. (Correct upto two decimal places)

 
NAT
Correct answer: 1.34
Solution

1.34 (1.20 - 1.50)

 
Q7 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Heat is flowing through a copper rod, steadily with rate of 3 kW. One end of the rod is at 150°C and the other is at 27°C. The rod is perfectly insulated along its length. The rate of entropy production due to irreversibility of this heat transfer is

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

 
Q8 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A copper block of 500 g mass and with cp = 150 J/K at 10°C is dropped from a height of 120 m into the lake at 10°C. The entropy change of the universe will be ______ J/K (Correct upto one decimal place)

NAT
Correct answer: 2.1
Solution

2.1(2 - 3)

 
Q9 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A thermally insulated 100 ohm resistor carries a current of 0.5 A for 2 sec. The initial temperature of the resistor is 7°C. Its mass is 2 g and its specific heat is 0.85 J/gK, change in the entropy of the resistor is

 
Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

 
Q10 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

In a steam generator, water is evaporated at 260°C, while the combustion gas cp = 1.08 kJ/kgK is cooled from 1500°C to 300°C. The surroundings are at 27°C. Latent heat of vapourization of water at 260°C = 1664 kJ/kg. The loss in available energy due to the heat transfer per kg of water evaporated will be ______ kJ. (Approx to nearest integer)

NAT
Correct answer: 467
Solution

467 (466 - 468)

 
Q11 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Air flows through an adiabatic compressor at 1 kg/s. The inlet condition are 1 bar and 27°C and the exit conditions are 8 bar and 600 K. The value of irreversibility will be _____ kW. Assume surrounding temperature to be 280 K (Correct upto two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 27.95
Solution

27.95 (26 - 28)

 
Q12 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Air expands steadily through a turbine from 10 bar, 900 K to 1 bar, 500 K. During the expansion, heat transfer from air to the surroundings at 300 K is 20 kJ/kg air. Neglect the chances in kinetic and potential energies. The irreversibility per kg air is _____ kJ/kg of air. Assume air to behave as an ideal gas with

 
Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

 
Q13 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Steam flows through an adiabatic flow turbine. The enthalpy at entrance is 4000 kJ/kg and at exit 2500 kJ/kg. The values of flow availability of steam at entrance and exit are 1800 kJ/kg and 150 kJ/kg respectively. If the dead state temperature T0 is 300 K, then change in entropy per kg of steam will be ______ kJ/kgK. Assume changes in kinetic and potential energy to be negligible.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

The specific volume of liquid water is 0.0010004 m3/kg and that of ice is 0.002081 m3/kg at 0.02°C. The latent heat of fusion of ice at this temperature is 334 kJ/kg. The rate of change of melting point of ice is _______ k/bar.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
